   127→# =============================================================================
   128→# AST Nodes
   129→# =============================================================================
   130→
   131→class ASTNode:
   132→    pass
   133→
   134→
   135→class SignalRef(ASTNode):
   136→    """Reference to a signal with optional slice/index.
   137→
   138→    name: signal name
   139→    msb: None for scalar reference, otherwise MSB of slice/index
   140→    lsb: None for scalar reference, LSB of slice (for single bit, msb=lsb)
   141→    """
   142→    def __init__(self, name: str, msb: Optional[int] = None, lsb: Optional[int] = None):
   143→        self.name = name
   144→        self.msb = msb
   145→        self.lsb = lsb
   146→
   147→    def __repr__(self):
   148→        if self.msb is None:
   149→            return f"SignalRef({self.name})"
   150→        elif self.msb == self.lsb:
   151→            return f"SignalRef({self.name}[{self.msb}])"
   152→        else:
   153→            return f"SignalRef({self.name}[{self.msb}:{self.lsb}])"
   154→
   155→
   156→class Literal(ASTNode):
   157→    """Literal value with width.
   158→
   159→    value: integer value
   160→    width: bit width
   161→    """
   162→    def __init__(self, value: int, width: int):
   163→        self.value = value
   164→        self.width = width
   165→
   166→    def __repr__(self):
   167→        return f"Literal(0b{self.value:0{self.width}b}, width={self.width})"
   168→
   169→
   170→class Concat(ASTNode):
   171→    """Concatenation: {e1, e2, ..., ek}"""
   172→    def __init__(self, parts: List[ASTNode]):
   173→        self.parts = parts
   174→
   175→    def __repr__(self):
   176→        return f"Concat([{', '.join(repr(p) for p in self.parts)}])"
   177→
   178→
   179→class OpCall(ASTNode):
   180→    """Operator call: OP(arg1, arg2, ...)"""
   181→    def __init__(self, op: str, args: List[ASTNode]):
   182→        self.op = op
   183→        self.args = args
   184→
   185→    def __repr__(self):
   186→        return f"OpCall({self.op}, [{', '.join(repr(a) for a in self.args)}])"
   187→
   188→
   189→# =============================================================================
   190→# Signal Declaration
   191→# =============================================================================
   192→
   193→class SignalDecl:
   194→    """Signal declaration with width information.
   195→
   196→    name: signal name
   197→    sig_type: 'input', 'output', or 'wire'
   198→    msb: MSB of the signal (None for scalar in old format)
   199→    lsb: LSB of the signal (None for scalar in old format)
   200→    """
   201→    def __init__(self, name: str, sig_type: str, msb: Optional[int] = None, lsb: Optional[int] = None):
   202→        self.name = name
   203→        self.sig_type = sig_type
   204→        self.msb = msb
   205→        self.lsb = lsb
   206→
   207→    @property
   208→    def width(self) -> int:
   209→        if self.msb is None:
   210→            return 1
   211→        return self.msb - self.lsb + 1
   212→
   213→    def __repr__(self):
   214→        if self.msb is None:
   215→            return f"SignalDecl({self.name}, {self.sig_type})"
   216→        return f"SignalDecl({self.name}[{self.msb}:{self.lsb}], {self.sig_type})"
   217→
   218→
   219→# =============================================================================
   220→# Token Types
   221→# =============================================================================
   222→
   223→TOKEN_ID = 'ID'
   224→TOKEN_KW = 'KW'
   225→TOKEN_OP = 'OP'
   226→TOKEN_LITERAL = 'LITERAL'
   227→TOKEN_NUMBER = 'NUMBER'
   228→TOKEN_LBRACE = '{'
   229→TOKEN_RBRACE = '}'
   230→TOKEN_LBRACK = '['
   231→TOKEN_RBRACK = ']'
   232→TOKEN_COLON = ':'
   233→TOKEN_EQ = '='
   234→TOKEN_COMMA = ','
   235→TOKEN_LPAREN = '('
   236→TOKEN_RPAREN = ')'
   237→TOKEN_EOF = 'EOF'
   238→
   239→
   240→class Token:
   241→    def __init__(self, type: str, value: str, line: int, col: int):
   242→        self.type = type
   243→        self.value = value
   244→        self.line = line
   245→        self.col = col
   246→
   247→    def __repr__(self):
   248→        return f"Token({self.type}, {self.value!r}, line={self.line}, col={self.col})"
   249→
   250→
   251→# =============================================================================
   252→# Lexer
   253→# =============================================================================
   254→
   255→def tokenize(line: str, line_num: int) -> List[Token]:
   256→    """Tokenize a single line.
   257→
   258→    Returns list of Token objects.
   259→    """
   260→    tokens = []
   261→    pos = 0
   262→    length = len(line)
   263→
   264→    # Operator patterns (case-insensitive)
   265→    operators = {'AND', 'OR', 'XOR', 'NAND', 'NOR', 'XNOR', 'NOT', 'BUF',
   266→                 'MUX', 'ITE', 'REDUCE_AND', 'REDUCE_OR', 'REDUCE_XOR', 'EQ'}
   267→
   268→    while pos < length:
   269→        col_pos = pos + 1  # 1-based column
   270→
   271→        # Skip whitespace
   272→        if line[pos] in ' \t':
   273→            pos += 1
   274→            continue
   275→
   276→        # Comment
   277→        if line[pos] == '#':
   278→            break  # Rest of line is comment, ignore
   279→
   280→        # Identifiers (letters, underscores, digits after first char)
   281→        if re.match(r'[a-zA-Z_]', line[pos:]):
   282→            match = re.match(r'[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*', line[pos:])
   283→            if match:
   284→                token = match.group()
   285→                upper_token = token.upper()
   286→                if upper_token in operators:
   287→                    tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_OP, upper_token, line_num, col_pos))
   288→                elif upper_token in {'INPUT', 'OUTPUT', 'WIRE'}:
   289→                    tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_KW, upper_token, line_num, col_pos))
   290→                else:
   291→                    tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_ID, token, line_num, col_pos))
   292→                pos += len(token)
   293→                continue
   294→
   295→        # Numbers (decimal integers for msb:lsb notation)
   296→        if re.match(r'[0-9]', line[pos:]):
   297→            match = re.match(r'[0-9]+', line[pos:])
   298→            if match:
   299→                tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_NUMBER, match.group(), line_num, col_pos))
   300→                pos += len(match.group())
   301→                continue
   302→
   303→        # Literals: 0, 1 (scalar), sized/unsized vectors
   304→        if line[pos] in '01':
   305→            # Check if this is a sized literal like 8'b1010
   306→            rest = line[pos:]
   307→            sized_match = re.match(r'(\d+)\'[bBhHdD]([\d01a-fA-F_]+)', rest)
   308→            if sized_match:
   309→                # This is a sized literal
   310→                width = int(sized_match.group(1))
   311→                radix_str = sized_match.group(2).lower()
   312→                value_str = sized_match.group(3).replace('_', '')
   313→
   314→                # Parse based on radix
   315→                if radix_str in ('b', 'h', 'd'):
   316→                    try:
   317→                        radix_map = {'b': 2, 'h': 16, 'd': 10}
   318→                        value = int(value_str, radix_map[radix_str])
   319→                    except ValueError:
   320→                        raise CircParseError(
   321→                            f"Invalid literal: {sized_match.group(0)}",
   322→                            file=None, line=line_num, col=col_pos
   323→                        )
   324→
   325→                    # Check value fits in width
   326→                    if value >= (1 << width):
   327→                        raise CircParseError(
   328→                            f"Value {value} too large for {width}-bit literal",
   329→                            file=None, line=line_num, col=col_pos
   330→                        )
   331→
   332→                    tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_LITERAL, f"{width}'{radix_str}{value_str}", line_num, col_pos))
   333→                    pos += len(sized_match.group(0))
   334→                    continue
   335→            else:
   336→                # Scalar literal
   337→                tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_LITERAL, line[pos], line_num, col_pos))
   338→                pos += 1
   339→                continue
   340→
   341→        # Check for 0b or 0x binary/hex literals
   342→        if line[pos:pos+2] in ('0b', '0x'):
   343→            prefix = line[pos:pos+2]
   344→            rest = line[pos+2:]
   345→            # Must have at least one digit after prefix
   346→            match = re.match(r'[0-9a-fA-F]+', rest)
   347→            if match:
   348→                digits = match.group()
   349→                tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_LITERAL, f"{prefix}{digits}", line_num, col_pos))
   350→                pos += 2 + len(digits)
   351→                continue
   352→            else:
   353→                raise CircParseError(
   354→                    f"Binary/hex literal must have at least one digit",
   355→                    file=None, line=line_num, col=col_pos
   356→                )
   357→
   358→        # Braces and brackets
   359→        if line[pos] in '{}[]':
   360→            tokens.append(Token(line[pos], line[pos], line_num, col_pos))
   361→            pos += 1
   362→            continue
   363→
   364→        # Colon
   365→        if line[pos] == ':':
   366→            tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_COLON, ':', line_num, col_pos))
   367→            pos += 1
   368→            continue
   369→
   370→        # Comma
   371→        if line[pos] == ',':
   372→            tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_COMMA, ',', line_num, col_pos))
   373→            pos += 1
   374→            continue
   375→
   376→        # Equals sign
   377→        if line[pos] == '=':
   378→            tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_EQ, '=', line_num, col_pos))
   379→            pos += 1
   380→            continue
   381→
   382→        # Parentheses
   383→        if line[pos] in '()':
   384→            type_map = {'(': TOKEN_LPAREN, ')': TOKEN_RPAREN}
   385→            tokens.append(Token(type_map[line[pos]], line[pos], line_num, col_pos))
   386→            pos += 1
   387→            continue
   388→
   389→        # Unknown character
   390→        raise CircParseError(
   391→            f"Unexpected character: {line[pos]}",
   392→            file=None, line=line_num, col=col_pos
   393→        )
   394→
   395→    tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_EOF, '', line_num, len(line) + 1))
   396→    return tokens
   397→
   398→
   399→# =============================================================================
   400→# Parser
   401→# =============================================================================
   402→
   403→class Parser:
   404→    """Parser for circuit expressions."""
   405→
   406→    def __init__(self, tokens: List[Token], filepath: str):
   407→        self.tokens = tokens
   408→        self.filepath = filepath
   409→        self.pos = 0
   410→
   411→    def peek(self) -> Token:
   412→        return self.tokens[self.pos]
   413→
   414→    def consume(self, expected_type: Optional[str] = None,
   415→                expected_value: Optional[str] = None) -> Token:
   416→        tok = self.peek()
   417→        if expected_type is not None and tok.type != expected_type:
   418→            raise self.error(f"Expected token type {expected_type}, got {tok.type}")
   419→        if expected_value is not None and tok.value != expected_value:
   420→            raise self.error(f"Expected {expected_value}, got {tok.value}")
   421→        self.pos += 1
   422→        return tok
   423→
   424→    def error(self, message: str) -> CircParseError:
   425→        tok = self.peek()
   426→        return CircParseError(
   427→            message,
   428→            file=self.filepath,
   429→            line=tok.line,
   430→            col=tok.col
   431→        )
   432→
   433→    def parse_expression(self) -> ASTNode:
   434→        """Parse an expression: concatenation, signal ref, literal, or op call."""
   435→        tok = self.peek()
   436→
   437→        # If it's an identifier followed by [, it's a slice/index
   438→        if tok.type == TOKEN_ID:
   439→            name = tok.value
   440→            self.consume(TOKEN_ID)
   441→
   442→            # Check for slice or index
   443→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_LBRACK:
   444→                return self.parse_signal_ref_with_select(name)
   445→            else:
   446→                return SignalRef(name)
   447→
   448→        elif tok.type == TOKEN_LITERAL:
   449→            return self.parse_literal()
   450→
   451→        elif tok.type == TOKEN_LBRACE:
   452→            # Concatenation: {e1, e2, ...}
   453→            return self.parse_concat()
   454→
   455→        elif tok.type == TOKEN_OP:
   456→            # Operator call: OP(...)
   457→            return self.parse_op_call()
   458→
   459→        else:
   460→            raise self.error(f"Unexpected token in expression: {tok.value}")
   461→
   462→    def parse_signal_ref_with_select(self, name: str) -> SignalRef:
   463→        """Parse a signal reference with optional slice/index: v[i] or v[hi:lo]"""
   464→        self.consume(TOKEN_LBRACK)
   465→
   466→        # Parse first number (msb or index)
   467→        if self.peek().type != TOKEN_NUMBER:
   468→            raise self.error("Expected number in slice/index")
   469→        msb_val = int(self.consume().value)
   470→
   471→        # Check if it's a slice (with colon) or single bit index
   472→        if self.peek().type == TOKEN_COLON:
   473→            self.consume(TOKEN_COLON)  # consume ':'
   474→
   475→            # Parse lsb
   476→            if self.peek().type != TOKEN_NUMBER:
   477→                raise self.error("Expected number after colon in slice")
   478→            lsb_val = int(self.consume().value)
   479→
   480→            self.consume(TOKEN_RBRACK)
   481→
   482→            # Validate: msb >= lsb >= 0
   483→            if msb_val < lsb_val:
   484→                raise self.error(f"Invalid slice: msb ({msb_val}) < lsb ({lsb_val})")
   485→            if lsb_val < 0:
   486→                raise self.error(f"Invalid slice: lsb ({lsb_val}) < 0")
   487→
   488→            return SignalRef(name, msb_val, lsb_val)
   489→        else:
   490→            # Single bit index
   491→            self.consume(TOKEN_RBRACK)
   492→            return SignalRef(name, msb_val, msb_val)  # msb=lsb for single bit
   493→
   494→    def parse_literal(self) -> Literal:
   495→        """Parse a literal value."""
   496→        tok = self.consume(TOKEN_LITERAL)
   497→        val = tok.value
   498→
   499→        if val in ('0', '1'):
   500→            return Literal(int(val), 1)
   501→        elif val.startswith('0b'):
   502→            # Binary literal: 0b1101
   503→            bits = val[2:]
   504→            if not bits:
   505→                raise self.error("Binary literal must have at least one digit")
   506→            value = int(bits, 2)
   507→            return Literal(value, len(bits))
   508→        elif val.startswith('0x'):
   509→            # Hex literal: 0x0f
   510→            hex_digits = val[2:]
   511→            if not hex_digits:
   512→                raise self.error("Hex literal must have at least one digit")
   513→            value = int(hex_digits, 16)
   514→            return Literal(value, len(hex_digits) * 4)
   515→        else:
   516→            # Sized literal: 8'b1010, 8'hff, 8'd200
   517→            match = re.match(r'(\d+)\'[bBhHdD]([\d01a-fA-F_]+)', val)
   518→            if match:
   519→                width = int(match.group(1))
   520→                radix_str = match.group(2).lower()
   521→                value_str = match.group(3).replace('_', '')
   522→
   523→                try:
   524→                    radix_map = {'b': 2, 'h': 16, 'd': 10}
   525→                    value = int(value_str, radix_map[radix_str])
   526→                except ValueError:
   527→                    raise self.error(f"Invalid literal: {val}")
   528→
   529→                if value >= (1 << width):
   530→                    raise self.error(f"Value {value} too large for {width}-bit literal")
   531→
   532→                return Literal(value, width)
   533→            else:
   534→                raise self.error(f"Invalid literal format: {val}")
   535→
   536→    def parse_concat(self) -> Concat:
   537→        """Parse concatenation: {e1, e2, ...}"""
   538→        self.consume(TOKEN_LBRACE)
   539→
   540→        parts = []
   541→        while True:
   542→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_RBRACE:
   543→                break
   544→
   545→            parts.append(self.parse_expression())
   546→
   547→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_COMMA:
   548→                self.consume(TOKEN_COMMA)
   549→                continue
   550→            elif self.peek().type == TOKEN_RBRACE:
   551→                break
   552→
   553→        self.consume(TOKEN_RBRACE)
   554→        return Concat(parts)
   555→
   556→    def parse_op_call(self) -> OpCall:
   557→        """Parse operator call: OP(arg1, arg2, ...)"""
   558→        op_tok = self.consume(TOKEN_OP)
   559→        op = op_tok.value
   560→
   561→        self.consume(TOKEN_LPAREN)
   562→
   563→        args = []
   564→        while True:
   565→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_RPAREN:
   566→                break
   567→
   568→            args.append(self.parse_expression())
   569→
   570→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_COMMA:
   571→                self.consume(TOKEN_COMMA)
   572→                continue
   573→            elif self.peek().type == TOKEN_RPAREN:
   574→                break
   575→
   576→        self.consume(TOKEN_RPAREN)
   577→        return OpCall(op, args)
   578→
   579→    def parse_assignment_line(self) -> Tuple[str, ASTNode]:
   580→        """Parse an assignment line: lhs = expression"""
   581→        # Parse LHS (identifier)
   582→        lhs_tok = self.consume(TOKEN_ID)
   583→        lhs = lhs_tok.value
   584→
   585→        self.consume(TOKEN_EQ)
   586→
   587→        # Parse RHS expression
   588→        expr = self.parse_expression()
   589→
   590→        # Make sure we're at EOF
   591→        if self.peek().type != TOKEN_EOF:
   592→            raise self.error(f"Unexpected token after expression: {self.peek().value}")
   593→
   594→        return lhs, expr
   595→
   596→    def parse_declaration(self) -> List[SignalDecl]:
   597→        """Parse a declaration line: INPUT/OUTPUT/WIRE name[msb:lsb]"""
   598→        kw_tok = self.consume(TOKEN_KW)
   599→        decl_type = kw_tok.value.lower()
   600→
   601→        decls = []
   602→        while True:
   603→            # Parse identifier
   604→            id_tok = self.consume(TOKEN_ID)
   605→            name = id_tok.value
   606→
   607→            # Check for width specifier
   608→            msb = None
   609→            lsb = None
   610→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_LBRACK:
   611→                self.consume(TOKEN_LBRACK)
   612→
   613→                # Parse msb
   614→                if self.peek().type != TOKEN_NUMBER:
   615→                    raise self.error(f"Expected number after [ for {name}")
   616→                msb = int(self.consume().value)
   617→
   618→                # Check for slice or single bit
   619→                if self.peek().type == TOKEN_COLON:
   620→                    self.consume(TOKEN_COLON)
   621→
   622→                    if self.peek().type != TOKEN_NUMBER:
   623→                        raise self.error(f"Expected number after : for {name}")
   624→                    lsb = int(self.consume().value)
   625→
   626→                    if msb < lsb:
   627→                        raise self.error(f"Invalid range for {name}: msb ({msb}) < lsb ({lsb})")
   628→                    if lsb < 0:
   629→                        raise self.error(f"Invalid range for {name}: lsb ({lsb}) < 0")
   630→                else:
   631→                    # Single bit - msb=lsb
   632→                    lsb = msb
   633→
   634→                self.consume(TOKEN_RBRACK)
   635→
   636→            decls.append(SignalDecl(name, decl_type, msb, lsb))
   637→
   638→            # Check for more names or end of line
   639→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_EOF:
   640→                break
   641→            elif self.peek().type == TOKEN_COMMA:
   642→                self.consume(TOKEN_COMMA)
   643→                continue
   644→            elif self.peek().type == TOKEN_ID:
   645→                # Another identifier directly - continue to consume it
   646→                continue
   647→            else:
   648→                raise self.error(f"Unexpected token: {self.peek().value}")
   649→
   650→        return decls
   651→
   652→
   653→def parse_circ_file(filepath: str) -> Tuple[List[SignalDecl], List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]]]:
   654→    """Parse a .circ file and return (declarations, assignments).
   655→
   656→    Returns:
   657→        declarations: List of SignalDecl objects
   658→        assignments: List of (lhs, expression) tuples
   659→    """
   660→    with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
   661→        lines = f.readlines()
   662→
   663→    declarations = []
   664→    assignments = []
   665→    seen_assignment = False
   666→
   667→    for line_num, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
   668→        line = line.rstrip('\n\r')
   669→
   670→        # Skip empty lines and comments
   671→        stripped = line.lstrip()
   672→        if not stripped or stripped.startswith('#'):
   673→            continue
   674→
   675→        try:
   676→            tokens = tokenize(line, line_num)
   677→        except CircParseError as e:
   678→            if e.file is None:
   679→                e.file = filepath
   680→            raise
   681→
   682→        if not tokens:
   683→            continue
   684→
   685→        parser = Parser(tokens, filepath)
   686→
   687→        # Check if it's a declaration
   688→        if tokens[0].type == TOKEN_KW:
   689→            # Declaration line
   690→            if seen_assignment:
   691→                raise DeclarationAfterAssignmentError(
   692→                    f"Declaration found after assignment",
   693→                    file=filepath,
   694→                    line=line_num,
   695→                    col=1
   696→                )
   697→
   698→            try:
   699→                decls = parser.parse_declaration()
   700→                declarations.extend(decls)
   701→            except CircParseError as e:
   702→                if e.file is None:
   703→                    e.file = filepath
   704→                raise
   705→
   706→        elif tokens[0].type == TOKEN_ID:
   707→            # Assignment line
   708→            seen_assignment = True
   709→
   710→            try:
   711→                lhs, expr = parser.parse_assignment_line()
   712→                assignments.append((lhs, expr))
   713→            except CircParseError as e:
   714→                if e.file is None:
   715→                    e.file = filepath
   716→                raise
   717→        else:
   718→            raise CircParseError(
   719→                f"Expected keyword (input/output/wire) or identifier",
   720→                file=filepath,
   721→                line=line_num,
   722→                col=1
   723→            )
   724→
   725→    return declarations, assignments
   726→

   729→# JSON Circuit Format Parser
   730→# =============================================================================
   731→
   732→def parse_json_file(filepath: str) -> Tuple[List[SignalDecl], List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]]]:
   733→    """Parse a JSON circuit file and return (declarations, assignments).
   734→
   735→    Returns:
   736→        declarations: List of SignalDecl objects
   737→        assignments: List of (lhs, expression) tuples
   738→
   739→    Raises:
   740→        JsonParseError: Invalid JSON syntax
   741→        JsonSchemaError: JSON doesn't match expected schema
   742→        CircParseError: Expression parsing error
   743→    """
   744→    with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
   745→        try:
   746→            data = json.load(f)
   747→        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
   748→            raise JsonParseError(
   749→                f"Invalid JSON syntax: {e}",
   750→                file=filepath,
   751→                line=e.lineno,
   752→                col=e.colno
   753→            )
   754→
   755→    # Validate schema
   756→    if not isinstance(data, dict):
   757→        raise JsonSchemaError(
   758→            "JSON root must be an object",
   759→            file=filepath
   760→        )
   761→
   762→    required_keys = {'format_version', 'inputs', 'outputs', 'wires', 'assignments'}
   763→    missing = required_keys - set(data.keys())
   764→    if missing:
   765→        raise JsonSchemaError(
   766→            f"Missing required keys: {', '.join(sorted(missing))}",
   767→            file=filepath
   768→        )
   769→
   770→    # Check format_version
   771→    if data['format_version'] != 1:
   772→        raise JsonSchemaError(
   773→            f"Unsupported format_version: {data['format_version']}. Expected 1.",
   774→            file=filepath
   775→        )
   776→
   777→    # Helper to validate port object
   778→    def validate_port(port: dict, key_name: str) -> dict:
   779→        if not isinstance(port, dict):
   780→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   781→                f"{key_name} must be an array of objects",
   782→                file=filepath
   783→            )
   784→        if 'name' not in port:
   785→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   786→                f"Port object missing 'name' field",
   787→                file=filepath
   788→            )
   789→        msb = port.get('msb')
   790→        lsb = port.get('lsb')
   791→
   792→        if msb is None and lsb is None:
   793→            # Scalar port - valid
   794→            return port
   795→
   796→        if not isinstance(msb, int) or not isinstance(lsb, int):
   797→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   798→                f"Port '{port.get('name', '?')}': msb and lsb must be integers",
   799→                file=filepath
   800→            )
   801→
   802→        if msb < lsb:
   803→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   804→                f"Port '{port['name']}': msb ({msb}) < lsb ({lsb})",
   805→                file=filepath
   806→            )
   807→
   808→        if lsb < 0:
   809→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   810→                f"Port '{port['name']}': lsb ({lsb}) < 0",
   811→                file=filepath
   812→            )
   813→
   814→        return port
   815→
   816→    # Parse inputs
   817→    inputs_list = data['inputs']
   818→    if not isinstance(inputs_list, list):
   819→        raise JsonSchemaError(
   820→            "'inputs' must be an array",
   821→            file=filepath
   822→        )
   823→
   824→    declarations: List[SignalDecl] = []
   825→    seen_names: Set[str] = set()
   826→
   827→    for i, port in enumerate(inputs_list):
   828→        validate_port(port, f"inputs[{i}]")
   829→        name = port['name']
   830→        if name in seen_names:
   831→            raise DuplicateNameError(
   832→                f"Duplicate input name: {name}",
   833→                file=filepath
   834→            )
   835→        seen_names.add(name)
   836→
   837→        msb = port.get('msb')
   838→        lsb = port.get('lsb')
   839→        # Convert None/None to None, None for scalar; otherwise keep values
   840→        msb_val = msb if msb is not None else None
   841→        lsb_val = lsb if lsb is not None else None
   842→        declarations.append(SignalDecl(name, 'input', msb_val, lsb_val))
   843→
   844→    # Parse outputs
   845→    outputs_list = data['outputs']
   846→    if not isinstance(outputs_list, list):
   847→        raise JsonSchemaError(
   848→            "'outputs' must be an array",
   849→            file=filepath
   850→        )
   851→
   852→    for i, port in enumerate(outputs_list):
   853→        validate_port(port, f"outputs[{i}]")
   854→        name = port['name']
   855→        if name in seen_names:
   856→            raise DuplicateNameError(
   857→                f"Duplicate output name: {name}",
   858→                file=filepath
   859→            )
   860→        seen_names.add(name)
   861→
   862→        msb = port.get('msb')
   863→        lsb = port.get('lsb')
   864→        msb_val = msb if msb is not None else None
   865→        lsb_val = lsb if lsb is not None else None
   866→        declarations.append(SignalDecl(name, 'output', msb_val, lsb_val))
   867→
   868→    # Parse wires
   869→    wires_list = data['wires']
   870→    if not isinstance(wires_list, list):
   871→        raise JsonSchemaError(
   872→            "'wires' must be an array",
   873→            file=filepath
   874→        )
   875→
   876→    for i, port in enumerate(wires_list):
   877→        validate_port(port, f"wires[{i}]")
   878→        name = port['name']
   879→        if name in seen_names:
   880→            raise DuplicateNameError(
   881→                f"Duplicate wire name: {name}",
   882→                file=filepath
   883→            )
   884→        seen_names.add(name)
   885→
   886→        msb = port.get('msb')
   887→        lsb = port.get('lsb')
   888→        msb_val = msb if msb is not None else None
   889→        lsb_val = lsb if lsb is not None else None
   890→        declarations.append(SignalDecl(name, 'wire', msb_val, lsb_val))
   891→
   892→    # Parse assignments
   893→    assignments_list = data['assignments']
   894→    if not isinstance(assignments_list, list):
   895→        raise JsonSchemaError(
   896→            "'assignments' must be an array",
   897→            file=filepath
   898→        )
   899→
   900→    assignments: List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]] = []
   901→    lhs_seen: Set[str] = set()
   902→
   903→    for i, assn in enumerate(assignments_list):
   904→        if not isinstance(assn, dict):
   905→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   906→                f"assignments[{i}] must be an object",
   907→                file=filepath
   908→            )
   909→        if 'lhs' not in assn:
   910→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   911→                f"assignments[{i}] missing 'lhs' field",
   912→                file=filepath
   913→            )
   914→        if 'rhs' not in assn:
   915→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   916→                f"assignments[{i}] missing 'rhs' field",
   917→                file=filepath
   918→            )
   919→
   920→        lhs = assn['lhs']
   921→        rhs_str = assn['rhs']
   922→
   923→        if not isinstance(lhs, str):
   924→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   925→                f"assignments[{i}].lhs must be a string",
   926→                file=filepath
   927→            )
   928→        if not isinstance(rhs_str, str):
   929→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   930→                f"assignments[{i}].rhs must be a string",
   931→                file=filepath
   932→            )
   933→
   934→        if lhs in lhs_seen:
   935→            raise RedefinitionError(
   936→                f"Signal '{lhs}' defined multiple times",
   937→                file=filepath
   938→            )
   939→        lhs_seen.add(lhs)
   940→
   941→        # Parse RHS expression using existing expression parser
   942→        # Create a temporary tokens list by tokenizing the expression string
   943→        # We'll reuse the existing tokenize function by creating a mock line
   944→        try:
   945→            tokens = tokenize(rhs_str, 1)  # line 1 for expression error context
   946→        except CircParseError as e:
   947→            # Adjust line/col to point to the assignment
   948→            raise CircParseError(
   949→                f"Error in assignment {i}: {e.message}",
   950→                file=filepath,
   951→                line=None,
   952→                col=None
   953→            )
   954→
   955→        parser = Parser(tokens, filepath)
   956→        try:
   957→            expr = parser.parse_expression()
   958→        except CircParseError as e:
   959→            raise CircParseError(
   960→                f"Error in assignment {i}: {e.message}",
   961→                file=filepath
   962→            )
   963→
   964→        # Ensure we consumed all tokens (no trailing junk)
   965→        if parser.peek().type != TOKEN_EOF:
   966→            raise CircParseError(
   967→                f"Unexpected token after expression in assignment {i}",
   968→                file=filepath
   969→            )
   970→
   971→        assignments.append((lhs, expr))
   972→
   973→    # Validate that every wire and output appears exactly once as assignment LHS
   974→    wire_names = {d.name for d in declarations if d.sig_type == 'wire'}
   975→    output_names = {d.name for d in declarations if d.sig_type == 'output'}
   976→    assigned_names = set(lhs_seen)
   977→
   978→    unassigned = (wire_names | output_names) - assigned_names
   979→    if unassigned:
   980→        raise UnassignedSignalError(
   981→            f"Unassigned signals: {', '.join(sorted(unassigned))}",
   982→            file=filepath
   983→        )
   984→
   985→    # Check for extra assignments (LHS not declared)
   986→    declared_names = {d.name for d in declarations}
   987→    extra_assigned = assigned_names - declared_names
   988→    if extra_assigned:
   989→        raise UndefinedNameError(
   990→            f"Assignment to undeclared signal(s): {', '.join(sorted(extra_assigned))}",
   991→            file=filepath
   992→        )
   993→
   994→    return declarations, assignments
   995→

   998→# BENCH Circuit Format Parser
   999→# =============================================================================
  1000→
  1001→def parse_bench_file(filepath: str) -> Tuple[List[SignalDecl], List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]]]:
  1002→    """Parse a BENCH format circuit file and return (declarations, assignments).
  1003→
  1004→    BENCH is a scalar-only format commonly used in circuit benchmarks.
  1005→
  1006→    Returns:
  1007→        declarations: List of SignalDecl objects (all scalar)
  1008→        assignments: List of (lhs, expression) tuples
  1009→
  1010→    Raises:
  1011→        BenchParseError: Syntax error in .bench file
  1012→        RedefinitionError: Signal defined multiple times
  1013→        UndefinedNameError: Undefined name referenced
  1014→    """
  1015→    with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
  1016→        lines = f.readlines()
  1017→
  1018→    declarations: List[SignalDecl] = []
  1019→    assignments: List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]] = []
  1020→
  1021→    # Track all signals (inputs, wires, outputs)
  1022→    signal_names: Set[str] = set()
  1023→    input_names: Set[str] = set()
  1024→    output_names: Set[str] = set()
  1025→    wire_names: Set[str] = set()
  1026→
  1027→    # Track assigned signals (for RedefinitionError)
  1028→    assigned_lhs: Set[str] = set()
  1029→
  1030→    # For expression parsing
  1031→    op_arity = {
  1032→        'NOT': 1,
  1033→        'BUF': 1,
  1034→        'AND': 2,
  1035→        'OR': 2,
  1036→        'XOR': 2,
  1037→        'NAND': 2,
  1038→        'NOR': 2,
  1039→        'XNOR': 2,
  1040→    }
  1041→
  1042→    for line_num, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
  1043→        line = line.rstrip('\n\r')
  1044→
  1045→        # Strip comment
  1046→        if '#' in line:
  1047→            line = line[:line.index('#')]
  1048→
  1049→        # Skip empty lines
  1050→        stripped = line.strip()
  1051→        if not stripped:
  1052→            continue
  1053→
  1054→        # Remove all whitespace for easier parsing
  1055→        no_whitespace = ''.join(stripped.split())
  1056→
  1057→        # Check for INPUT(name)
  1058→        if no_whitespace.startswith('INPUT(') and no_whitespace.endswith(')'):
  1059→            name = no_whitespace[6:-1]  # Remove 'INPUT(' and ')'
  1060→            if not name:
  1061→                raise BenchParseError(
  1062→                    f"Empty input name at line {line_num}",
  1063→                    file=filepath,
  1064→                    line=line_num,
  1065→                    col=1
  1066→                )
  1067→
  1068→            # Check for brackets (vector support not allowed in BENCH)
  1069→            if '[' in name or ']' in name:
  1070→                raise BenchParseError(
  1071→                    f"Vector notation not allowed in BENCH: {name}",
  1072→                    file=filepath,
  1073→                    line=line_num,
  1074→                    col=1
  1075→                )
  1076→
  1077→            if name in signal_names:
  1078→                raise RedefinitionError(
  1079→                    f"Duplicate signal definition: {name}",
  1080→                    file=filepath,
  1081→                    line=line_num,
  1082→                    col=1
  1083→                )
  1084→
  1085→            signal_names.add(name)
  1086→            input_names.add(name)
  1087→            declarations.append(SignalDecl(name, 'input', None, None))
  1088→            continue
  1089→
  1090→        # Check for OUTPUT(name)
  1091→        if no_whitespace.startswith('OUTPUT(') and no_whitespace.endswith(')'):
  1092→            name = no_whitespace[7:-1]  # Remove 'OUTPUT(' and ')'
  1093→            if not name:
  1094→                raise BenchParseError(
  1095→                    f"Empty output name at line {line_num}",
  1096→                    file=filepath,
  1097→                    line=line_num,
  1098→                    col=1
  1099→                )
  1100→
  1101→            if '[' in name or ']' in name:
  1102→                raise BenchParseError(
  1103→                    f"Vector notation not allowed in BENCH: {name}",
  1104→                    file=filepath,
  1105→                    line=line_num,
  1106→                    col=1
  1107→                )
  1108→
  1109→            if name in signal_names:
  1110→                raise RedefinitionError(
  1111→                    f"Duplicate signal definition: {name}",
  1112→                    file=filepath,
  1113→                    line=line_num,
  1114→                    col=1
  1115→                )
  1116→
  1117→            signal_names.add(name)
  1118→            output_names.add(name)
  1119→            # Add as output declaration
  1120→            declarations.append(SignalDecl(name, 'output', None, None))
  1121→            continue
  1122→
  1123→        # Check for assignment: lhs = OP(arg1, arg2, ...)
  1124→        if '=' in no_whitespace:
  1125→            parts = no_whitespace.split('=', 1)
  1126→            if len(parts) != 2:
  1127→                raise BenchParseError(
  1128→                    f"Invalid assignment at line {line_num}",
  1129→                    file=filepath,
  1130→                    line=line_num,
  1131→                    col=1
  1132→                )
  1133→
  1134→            lhs = parts[0]
  1135→            rhs = parts[1]
  1136→
  1137→            # Validate LHS
  1138→            if not lhs:
  1139→                raise BenchParseError(
  1140→                    f"Empty LHS in assignment at line {line_num}",
  1141→                    file=filepath,
  1142→                    line=line_num,
  1143→                    col=1
  1144→                )
  1145→
  1146→            if '[' in lhs or ']' in lhs:
  1147→                raise BenchParseError(
  1148→                    f"Vector notation not allowed in BENCH: {lhs}",
  1149→                    file=filepath,
  1150→                    line=line_num,
  1151→                    col=1
  1152→                )
  1153→
  1154→            # Check for redefinition
  1155→            if lhs in assigned_lhs:
  1156→                raise RedefinitionError(
  1157→                    f"Redefinition of signal: {lhs}",
  1158→                    file=filepath,
  1159→                    line=line_num,
  1160→                    col=1
  1161→                )
  1162→
  1163→            assigned_lhs.add(lhs)
  1164→
  1165→            # Add as wire if not already declared as input/output
  1166→            if lhs not in signal_names:
  1167→                signal_names.add(lhs)
  1168→                wire_names.add(lhs)
  1169→                declarations.append(SignalDecl(lhs, 'wire', None, None))
  1170→
  1171→            # Parse RHS: OP(arg1, arg2, ...)
  1172→            # Find operator name
  1173→            op_match = None
  1174→            for op in op_arity:
  1175→                if rhs.startswith(op + '(') and rhs.endswith(')'):
  1176→                    op_match = op
  1177→                    break
  1178→
  1179→            if op_match is None:
  1180→                raise BenchParseError(
  1181→                    f"Unknown or malformed operator call: {rhs}",
  1182→                    file=filepath,
  1183→                    line=line_num,
  1184→                    col=1
  1185→                )
  1186→
  1187→            args_str = rhs[len(op_match)+1:-1]  # Remove OP( and )
  1188→            if args_str:
  1189→                arg_names = [arg.strip() for arg in args_str.split(',')]
  1190→            else:
  1191→                arg_names = []
  1192→
  1193→            # Validate argument count
  1194→            min_arity = op_arity[op_match]
  1195→            if len(arg_names) < min_arity:
  1196→                raise BenchParseError(
  1197→                    f"Operator {op_match} requires at least {min_arity} operands, got {len(arg_names)}",
  1198→                    file=filepath,
  1199→                    line=line_num,
  1200→                    col=1
  1201→                )
  1202→
  1203→            # Check for literals (BENCH doesn't allow literals in RHS)
  1204→            for arg in arg_names:
  1205→                # Literals: 0, 1, 0b..., 0x..., sized literals
  1206→                if arg in ('0', '1'):
  1207→                    raise BenchParseError(
  1208→                        f"Literals not allowed in BENCH: {arg}",
  1209→                        file=filepath,
  1210→                        line=line_num,
  1211→                        col=1
  1212→                    )
  1213→                if arg.startswith('0b') or arg.startswith('0x'):
  1214→                    raise BenchParseError(
  1215→                        f"Literals not allowed in BENCH: {arg}",
  1216→                        file=filepath,
  1217→                        line=line_num,
  1218→                        col=1
  1219→                    )
  1220→                if re.match(r'^\d+[\'[bBhHdD]', arg):
  1221→                    raise BenchParseError(
  1222→                        f"Literals not allowed in BENCH: {arg}",
  1223→                        file=filepath,
  1224→                        line=line_num,
  1225→                        col=1
  1226→                    )
  1227→
  1228→                # Check for brackets (vector not allowed)
  1229→                if '[' in arg or ']' in arg:
  1230→                    raise BenchParseError(
  1231→                        f"Vector notation not allowed in BENCH: {arg}",
  1232→                        file=filepath,
  1233→                        line=line_num,
  1234→                        col=1
  1235→                    )
  1236→
  1237→            # Build AST: OpCall with SignalRef arguments
  1238→            args_ast = []
  1239→            for arg in arg_names:
  1240→                # BENCH is scalar-only, no indices/slices
  1241→                args_ast.append(SignalRef(arg))
  1242→
  1243→            expr = OpCall(op_match, args_ast)
  1244→            assignments.append((lhs, expr))
  1245→            continue
  1246→
  1247→        # If we get here, the line is invalid
  1248→        raise BenchParseError(
  1249→            f"Invalid statement: {stripped}",
  1250→            file=filepath,
  1251→            line=line_num,
  1252→            col=1
  1253→        )
  1254→
  1255→    # Validate OUTPUT references
  1256→    for out_name in output_names:
  1257→        if out_name not in signal_names:
  1258→            raise UndefinedNameError(
  1259→                f"OUTPUT references undefined signal: {out_name}",
  1260→                file=filepath
  1261→            )
  1262→        # Also check that OUTPUT is assigned somewhere (either as input or assigned)
  1263→        if out_name not in assigned_lhs and out_name not in input_names:
  1264→            raise UndefinedNameError(
  1265→                f"OUTPUT references undefined signal: {out_name}",
  1266→                file=filepath
  1267→            )
  1268→
  1269→    # Validate that all assigned signals (except inputs) are defined
  1270→    for lhs in assigned_lhs:
  1271→        if lhs not in input_names and lhs not in wire_names:
  1272→            # Already added as wire, but check consistency
  1273→            pass
  1274→
  1275→    # Also: wires must be assigned (already tracked via assigned_lhs)
  1276→    for wname in wire_names:
  1277→        if wname not in assigned_lhs:
  1278→            raise UnassignedSignalError(
  1279→                f"Unassigned wire: {wname}",
  1280→                file=filepath
  1281→            )
  1282→
  1283→    return declarations, assignments
  1284→
  1285→
  1286→# =============================================================================
  1287→# Expression Evaluator (for circuit evaluation)
  1288→# =============================================================================
  1289→
  1290→def eval_expression(expr: ASTNode, signal_values: Dict[str, int],
  1291→                    signal_widths: Dict[str, int],
  1292→                    filepath: str) -> Tuple[int, int]:
  1293→    """Evaluate an expression and return (value, width).
  1294→
  1295→    Args:
  1296→        expr: AST node to evaluate
  1297→        signal_values: Dict mapping signal names to their integer values
  1298→        signal_widths: Dict mapping signal names to their widths
  1299→        filepath: For error reporting
  1300→
  1301→    Returns:
  1302→        Tuple of (value, width)
  1303→    """
  1304→    if isinstance(expr, SignalRef):
  1305→        if expr.name in signal_values:
  1306→            # Get the full signal value
  1307→            full_value = signal_values[expr.name]
  1308→            width = signal_widths[expr.name]
  1309→
  1310→            if expr.msb is None:
  1311→                # Scalar reference
  1312→                return full_value, 1
  1313→            else:
  1314→                # Slice or single bit
  1315→                if expr.msb >= width or expr.lsb >= width:
  1316→                    raise IndexOutOfBoundsError(
  1317→                        f"Index {expr.msb}:{expr.lsb} out of bounds for signal {expr.name} (width {width})",
  1318→                        file=filepath
  1319→                    )
  1320→                # Extract bits from lsb to msb
  1321→                mask = ((1 << (expr.msb - expr.lsb + 1)) - 1) << expr.lsb
  1322→                slice_value = (full_value & mask) >> expr.lsb
  1323→                return slice_value, expr.msb - expr.lsb + 1
  1324→        else:
  1325→            raise UndefinedNameError(
  1326→                f"Undefined signal: {expr.name}",
  1327→                file=filepath
  1328→            )
  1329→
  1330→    elif isinstance(expr, Literal):
  1331→        return expr.value, expr.width
  1332→
  1333→    elif isinstance(expr, Concat):
  1334→        # Evaluate all parts
  1335→        parts_values = []
  1336→        total_width = 0
  1337→        for part in expr.parts:
  1338→            val, width = eval_expression(part, signal_values, signal_widths, filepath)
  1339→            parts_values.append((val, width))
  1340→            total_width += width
  1341→
  1342→        # Concatenate: first part is MSB
  1343→        result = 0
  1344→        for val, width in parts_values:
  1345→            result = (result << width) | val
  1346→
  1347→        return result, total_width
  1348→
  1349→    elif isinstance(expr, OpCall):
  1350→        op = expr.op.upper()
  1351→        args_results = []
  1352→        for arg in expr.args:
  1353→            val, width = eval_expression(arg, signal_values, signal_widths, filepath)
  1354→            args_results.append((val, width))
  1355→
  1356→        # Bitwise operators (width-preserving)
  1357→        if op in ('NOT', 'BUF'):
  1358→            if len(args_results) != 1:
  1359→                raise WidthMismatchError(
  1360→                    f"Operator {op} requires 1 operand, got {len(args_results)}",
  1361→                    file=filepath
  1362→                )
  1363→            val, width = args_results[0]
  1364→            result = (~val) & ((1 << width) - 1) if op == 'NOT' else val
  1365→            return result, width
  1366→
  1367→        elif op in ('AND', 'OR', 'XOR', 'NAND', 'NOR', 'XNOR'):
  1368→            if len(args_results) < 2:
  1369→                raise WidthMismatchError(
  1370→                    f"Operator {op} requires at least 2 operands, got {len(args_results)}",
  1371→                    file=filepath
  1372→                )
  1373→
  1374→            # Check all operands have same width
  1375→            first_width = args_results[0][1]
  1376→            for val, width in args_results:
  1377→                if width != first_width:
  1378→                    raise WidthMismatchError(
  1379→                        f"Operator {op}: operand widths {width} and {first_width} don't match",
  1380→                        file=filepath
  1381→                    )
  1382→
  1383→            mask = (1 << first_width) - 1
  1384→            result = args_results[0][0]
  1385→
  1386→            if op == 'AND':
  1387→                for val, _ in args_results[1:]:
  1388→                    result &= val
  1389→            elif op == 'OR':
  1390→                for val, _ in args_results[1:]:
  1391→                    result |= val
  1392→            elif op == 'XOR':
  1393→                for val, _ in args_results[1:]:
  1394→                    result ^= val
  1395→            elif op == 'NAND':
  1396→                for val, _ in args_results[1:]:
  1397→                    result &= val
  1398→                result = mask & ~result
  1399→            elif op == 'NOR':
  1400→                for val, _ in args_results[1:]:
  1401→                    result |= val
  1402→                result = mask & ~result
  1403→            elif op == 'XNOR':
  1404→                for val, _ in args_results[1:]:
  1405→                    result ^= val
  1406→                result = mask & ~result
  1407→
  1408→            return result & mask, first_width
  1409→
  1410→        elif op in ('MUX', 'ITE'):
  1411→            if len(args_results) != 3:
  1412→                raise ArityError(
  1413→                    f"Operator {op} requires 3 operands, got {len(args_results)}",
  1414→                    file=filepath
  1415→                )
  1416→
  1417→            sel_val, sel_width = args_results[0]
  1418→            a_val, a_width = args_results[1]
  1419→            b_val, b_width = args_results[2]
  1420→
  1421→            if sel_width != 1:
  1422→                raise WidthMismatchError(
  1423→                    f"Operator {op}: selector width {sel_width} must be 1",
  1424→                    file=filepath
  1425→                )
  1426→            if a_width != b_width:
  1427→                raise WidthMismatchError(
  1428→                    f"Operator {op}: operand widths {a_width} and {b_width} don't match",
  1429→                    file=filepath
  1430→                )
  1431→
  1432→            result = a_val if sel_val else b_val
  1433→            return result, a_width
  1434→
  1435→        elif op in ('REDUCE_AND', 'REDUCE_OR', 'REDUCE_XOR'):
  1436→            if len(args_results) != 1:
  1437→                raise WidthMismatchError(
  1438→                    f"Operator {op} requires 1 operand, got {len(args_results)}",
  1439→                    file=filepath
  1440→                )
  1441→
  1442→            val, width = args_results[0]
  1443→            if width < 1:
  1444→                raise WidthMismatchError(
  1445→                    f"Operator {op}: operand width {width} must be >= 1",
  1446→                    file=filepath
  1447→                )
  1448→
  1449→            mask = (1 << width) - 1
  1450→            val &= mask
  1451→
  1452→            if op == 'REDUCE_AND':
  1453→                # Check if all bits are 1
  1454→                result = 1 if val == mask else 0
  1455→            elif op == 'REDUCE_OR':
  1456→                result = 1 if val != 0 else 0
  1457→            else:  # REDUCE_XOR
  1458→                result = bin(val & mask).count('1') % 2
  1459→
  1460→            return result, 1
  1461→
  1462→        elif op == 'EQ':
  1463→            if len(args_results) != 2:
  1464→                raise ArityError(
  1465→                    f"Operator EQ requires 2 operands, got {len(args_results)}",
  1466→                    file=filepath
  1467→                )
  1468→
  1469→            a_val, a_width = args_results[0]
  1470→            b_val, b_width = args_results[1]
  1471→
  1472→            if a_width != b_width:
  1473→                raise WidthMismatchError(
  1474→                    f"Operator EQ: operand widths {a_width} and {b_width} don't match",
  1475→                    file=filepath
  1476→                )
  1477→
  1478→            return int(a_val == b_val), 1
  1479→
  1480→        else:
  1481→            raise ArityError(
  1482→                f"Unknown operator: {op}",
  1483→                file=filepath
  1484→            )
  1485→
  1486→    else:
  1487→        raise ValueError(f"Unknown AST node type: {type(expr)}")
  1488→
  1489→
  1490→def parse_input_literal(value_str: str, expected_width: int,
  1491→                        filepath: str, line: int = None, col: int = None) -> int:
  1492→    """Parse an input literal value and return the integer value.
  1493→
  1494→    Validates that the width matches the expected width.
  1495→    """
  1496→    if value_str in ('0', '1'):
  1497→        if expected_width != 1:
  1498→            raise InputWidthMismatchError(
  1499→                f"Input width mismatch: got 1 but expected {expected_width}",
  1500→                file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1501→            )
  1502→        return int(value_str)
  1503→
  1504→    elif value_str.startswith('0b'):
  1505→        bits = value_str[2:]
  1506→        if not bits:
  1507→            raise InputValueParseError(
  1508→                f"Binary literal must have at least one digit",
  1509→                file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1510→            )
  1511→        value = int(bits, 2)
  1512→        width = len(bits)
  1513→
  1514→        if width != expected_width:
  1515→            raise InputWidthMismatchError(
  1516→                f"Input width mismatch: got {width} but expected {expected_width}",
  1517→                file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1518→            )
  1519→        return value
  1520→
  1521→    elif value_str.startswith('0x'):
  1522→        hex_digits = value_str[2:]
  1523→        if not hex_digits:
  1524→            raise InputValueParseError(
  1525→                f"Hex literal must have at least one digit",
  1526→                file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1527→            )
  1528→        value = int(hex_digits, 16)
  1529→        width = len(hex_digits) * 4
  1530→
  1531→        if width != expected_width:
  1532→            raise InputWidthMismatchError(
  1533→                f"Input width mismatch: got {width} but expected {expected_width}",
  1534→                file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1535→            )
  1536→        return value
  1537→
  1538→    else:
  1539→        # Sized literal: 8'b1010, 8'hff, 8'd200
  1540→        match = re.match(r'(\d+)\'[bBhHdD]([\d01a-fA-F_]+)', value_str)
  1541→        if match:
  1542→            width = int(match.group(1))
  1543→            radix_str = match.group(2).lower()
  1544→            value_str_clean = match.group(3).replace('_', '')
  1545→
  1546→            if width != expected_width:
  1547→                raise InputWidthMismatchError(
  1548→                    f"Input width mismatch: got {width} but expected {expected_width}",
  1549→                    file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1550→                )
  1551→
  1552→            try:
  1553→                if radix_str == 'b':
  1554→                    value = int(value_str_clean, 2)
  1555→                elif radix_str == 'h':
  1556→                    value = int(value_str_clean, 16)
  1557→                else:  # 'd'
  1558→                    value = int(value_str_clean, 10)
  1559→            except ValueError:
  1560→                raise InputValueParseError(
  1561→                    f"Invalid literal: {value_str}",
  1562→                    file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1563→                )
  1564→
  1565→            if value >= (1 << width):
  1566→                raise CircParseError(
  1567→                    f"Value {value} too large for {width}-bit literal",
  1568→                    file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1569→                )
  1570→
  1571→            return value
  1572→        else:
  1573→            raise InputValueParseError(
  1574→                f"Invalid input literal format: {value_str}",
  1575→                file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1576→            )
  1577→
  1578→
  1579→def format_vector_value(value: int, width: int, radix: str) -> str:
  1580→    """Format a vector value according to the specified radix.
  1581→
  1582→    Args:
  1583→        value: Integer value
  1584→        width: Bit width
  1585→        radix: 'bin', 'hex', or 'dec'
  1586→
  1587→    Returns:
  1588→        Formatted string
  1589→    """
  1590→    value &= (1 << width) - 1
  1591→
  1592→    if radix == 'bin':
  1593→        return f"0b{value:0{width}b}"
  1594→    elif radix == 'hex':
  1595→        # Calculate minimum number of hex digits
  1596→        hex_digits = (width + 3) // 4
  1597→        return f"0x{value:0{hex_digits}x}"
  1598→    elif radix == 'dec':
  1599→        return str(value)
  1600→

  1602→# =============================================================================
  1603→# Validation
  1604→# =============================================================================
  1605→
  1606→def validate_circ_file(filepath: str, declarations: List[SignalDecl],
  1607→                       assignments: List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]]) -> Dict[str, object]:
  1608→    """Validate declarations and assignments, compute widths."""
  1609→
  1610→    # Track all declared signals
  1611→    inputs: Dict[str, SignalDecl] = {}
  1612→    outputs: Dict[str, SignalDecl] = {}
  1613→    wires: Dict[str, SignalDecl] = {}
  1614→    all_signals: Dict[str, SignalDecl] = {}
  1615→
  1616→    # Process declarations
  1617→    for decl in declarations:
  1618→        if decl.name in all_signals:
  1619→            raise DuplicateNameError(
  1620→                f"Duplicate signal name: {decl.name}",
  1621→                file=filepath
  1622→            )
  1623→        all_signals[decl.name] = decl
  1624→        if decl.sig_type == 'input':
  1625→            inputs[decl.name] = decl
  1626→        elif decl.sig_type == 'output':
  1627→            outputs[decl.name] = decl
  1628→        elif decl.sig_type == 'wire':
  1629→            wires[decl.name] = decl
  1630→
  1631→    # Track assignments
  1632→    assigned: Dict[str, Tuple[str, int]] = {}  # name -> (op, line_num)
  1633→
  1634→    # Build dependency graph for cycle detection
  1635→    graph: Dict[str, List[str]] = {}
  1636→
  1637→    # Operator arities
  1638→    op_arity = {
  1639→        'NOT': 1,
  1640→        'BUF': 1,
  1641→        'AND': 2,
  1642→        'OR': 2,
  1643→        'XOR': 2,
  1644→        'NAND': 2,
  1645→        'NOR': 2,
  1646→        'XNOR': 2,
  1647→        'MUX': 3,
  1648→        'ITE': 3,
  1649→        'REDUCE_AND': 1,
  1650→        'REDUCE_OR': 1,
  1651→        'REDUCE_XOR': 1,
  1652→        'EQ': 2,
  1653→    }
  1654→
  1655→    for i, (lhs, expr) in enumerate(assignments):
  1656→        line_num = i + 1
  1657→
  1658→        # Check LHS is valid
  1659→        if lhs not in all_signals:
  1660→            raise UndefinedNameError(
  1661→                f"Undefined signal: {lhs}",
  1662→                file=filepath
  1663→            )
  1664→
  1665→        signal_decl = all_signals[lhs]
  1666→        if signal_decl.sig_type == 'input':
  1667→            raise InputAssignmentError(
  1668→                f"Cannot assign to input: {lhs}",
  1669→                file=filepath
  1670→            )
  1671→
  1672→        # Check for multiple assignment
  1673→        if lhs in assigned:
  1674→            raise MultipleAssignmentError(
  1675→                f"Multiple assignment to: {lhs}",
  1676→                file=filepath
  1677→            )
  1678→
  1679→        assigned[lhs] = ('expr', line_num)
  1680→
  1681→        # Check expression and collect dependencies
  1682→        graph[lhs] = []
  1683→
  1684→        def collect_deps(node: ASTNode):
  1685→            if isinstance(node, SignalRef):
  1686→                if node.msb is not None:  # Has slice
  1687→                    # Check bounds against declared width if available
  1688→                    if node.name in all_signals:
  1689→                        decl = all_signals[node.name]
  1690→                        width = decl.width
  1691→                        if node.msb >= width or node.lsb >= width:
  1692→                            raise IndexOutOfBoundsError(
  1693→                                f"Index {node.msb}:{node.lsb} out of bounds for signal {node.name} (width {width})",
  1694→                                file=filepath
  1695→                            )
  1696→                if node.name not in ('0', '1'):
  1697→                    graph[lhs].append(node.name)
  1698→            elif isinstance(node, Literal):
  1699→                pass  # literals have no deps
  1700→            elif isinstance(node, Concat):
  1701→                for part in node.parts:
  1702→                    collect_deps(part)
  1703→            elif isinstance(node, OpCall):
  1704→                # Check arity
  1705→                op_upper = node.op.upper()
  1706→                if op_upper in op_arity:
  1707→                    min_arity = op_arity[op_upper]
  1708→                    if len(node.args) < min_arity:
  1709→                        raise ArityError(
  1710→                            f"Operator {node.op} requires at least {min_arity} operands, got {len(node.args)}",
  1711→                            file=filepath
  1712→                        )
  1713→                else:
  1714→                    raise ArityError(
  1715→                        f"Unknown operator: {node.op}",
  1716→                        file=filepath
  1717→                    )
  1718→                for arg in node.args:
  1719→                    collect_deps(arg)
  1720→
  1721→        try:
  1722→            collect_deps(expr)
  1723→        except (IndexOutOfBoundsError, ArityError):
  1724→            raise
  1725→        except Exception as e:
  1726→            raise CircParseError(
  1727→                f"Error in expression: {e}",
  1728→                file=filepath
  1729→            )
  1730→
  1731→    # Check for unassigned signals (only wires and outputs)
  1732→    for name, sig_type in [('wire', wires), ('output', outputs)]:
  1733→        for decl in all_signals.values():
  1734→            if decl.sig_type in ('wire', 'output'):
  1735→                if decl.name not in assigned:
  1736→                    raise UnassignedSignalError(
  1737→                        f"Unassigned signal: {decl.name}",
  1738→                        file=filepath
  1739→                    )
  1740→
  1741→    # Check for cycles
  1742→    def find_cycle() -> Optional[List[str]]:
  1743→        visited: Set[str] = set()
  1744→        rec_stack: List[str] = []
  1745→
  1746→        def dfs(node: str) -> Optional[List[str]]:
  1747→            visited.add(node)
  1748→            rec_stack.append(node)
  1749→
  1750→            for neighbor in graph.get(node, []):
  1751→                if neighbor not in visited:
  1752→                    result = dfs(neighbor)
  1753→                    if result:
  1754→                        return result
  1755→                elif neighbor in rec_stack:
  1756→                    start_idx = rec_stack.index(neighbor)
  1757→                    return rec_stack[start_idx:] + [node]
  1758→
  1759→            rec_stack.pop()
  1760→            return None
  1761→
  1762→        for node in graph:
  1763→            if node not in visited:
  1764→                cycle = dfs(node)
  1765→                if cycle:
  1766→                    return cycle
  1767→        return None
  1768→
  1769→    cycle = find_cycle()
  1770→    if cycle:
  1771→        cycle_path = " -> ".join(cycle)
  1772→        raise CycleError(
  1773→            f"Cycle detected: {cycle_path}",
  1774→            file=filepath
  1775→        )
  1776→
  1777→    # Return success info
  1778→    sorted_inputs = sorted(inputs.values(), key=lambda d: d.name)
  1779→    sorted_outputs = sorted(outputs.values(), key=lambda d: d.name)
  1780→
  1781→    return {
  1782→        'inputs': [{'name': d.name, 'msb': d.msb, 'lsb': d.lsb} for d in sorted_inputs],
  1783→        'outputs': [{'name': d.name, 'msb': d.msb, 'lsb': d.lsb} for d in sorted_outputs],
  1784→    }
  1785→

  1791→def format_error_json(error: CircuitError, command: str) -> str:
  1792→    """Format error as JSON envelope."""
  1793→    error_type = type(error).__name__
  1794→    result = {
  1795→        'ok': False,
  1796→        'command': command,
  1797→        'exit_code': error.exit_code,
  1798→        'error': {
  1799→            'type': error_type,
  1800→            'message': error.message,
  1801→            'file': error.file,
  1802→            'line': error.line,
  1803→            'col': error.col,
  1804→        }
  1805→    }
  1806→    return json.dumps(result)
  1807→

  1809→def format_version_json() -> str:
  1810→    """Format version output as JSON."""
  1811→    return json.dumps({'ok': True, 'command': '__version__', 'version': VERSION})

  1813→
  1814→def detect_format(filepath: str, format_override: str) -> str:
  1815→    """Detect circuit format from file extension or override.
  1816→
  1817→    Args:
  1818→        filepath: Path to the circuit file
  1819→        format_override: One of 'auto', 'circ', 'json', 'bench'
  1820→
  1821→    Returns:
  1822→        Format string: 'circ', 'json', or 'bench'
  1823→
  1824→    Raises:
  1825→        UnknownInputFormatError: If format cannot be determined
  1826→    """
  1827→    if format_override != 'auto':
  1828→        return format_override
  1829→
  1830→    # Determine from file extension
  1831→    _, ext = os.path.splitext(filepath)
  1832→    ext = ext.lower()
  1833→
  1834→    if ext == '.circ':
  1835→        return 'circ'

  1916→def get_dependencies(expr: ASTNode) -> List[str]:
  1917→    """Extract signal dependencies from an expression recursively.
  1918→
  1919→    For indexed/sliced references, the dependency is on the base signal.
  1920→    Returns a list of signal names (with duplicates).
  1921→    """
  1922→    deps = []
  1923→
  1924→    if isinstance(expr, SignalRef):
  1925→        deps.append(expr.name)
  1926→    elif isinstance(expr, Concat):
  1927→        for part in expr.parts:
  1928→            deps.extend(get_dependencies(part))
  1929→    elif isinstance(expr, OpCall):
  1930→        for arg in expr.args:
  1931→            deps.extend(get_dependencies(arg))
  1932→
  1933→    return deps
  1934→
  1935→
  1936→def classify_expression(expr: ASTNode) -> str:
  1937→    """Classify an expression for op_histogram categories."""
  1938→    if isinstance(expr, SignalRef):
  1939→        return "BUF"
  1940→    elif isinstance(expr, Literal):
  1941→        return "BUF"
  1942→    elif isinstance(expr, Concat):
  1943→        return "CONCAT"
  1944→    elif isinstance(expr, OpCall):
  1945→        return expr.op.upper()
  1946→    else:
  1947→        return "BUF"
  1948→
  1949→
  1950→def compute_depth(declarations: List[SignalDecl],
  1951→                  assignments: List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]],
  1952→                  inputs: Dict[str, SignalDecl],
  1953→                  outputs: Dict[str, SignalDecl]) -> int:
  1954→    """Compute the maximum depth from any input to any output."""
  1955→    # Build a map of assignment LHS to its depth
  1956→    # For each assignment: depth = 1 + max(depth of dependencies)
  1957→    # If no dependencies (BUF from input/literal), depth = 1
  1958→
  1959→    # Build dependency graph
  1960→    graph: Dict[str, List[str]] = {}
  1961→    dep_counts: Dict[str, int] = {}  # Track number of dependencies
  1962→
  1963→    def collect_deps(node: ASTNode):
  1964→        if isinstance(node, SignalRef):
  1965→            return [node.name]
  1966→        elif isinstance(node, Concat):
  1967→            deps = []
  1968→            for part in node.parts:
  1969→                deps.extend(collect_deps(part))
  1970→            return deps
  1971→        elif isinstance(node, OpCall):
  1972→            deps = []
  1973→            for arg in node.args:
  1974→                deps.extend(collect_deps(arg))
  1975→            return deps
  1976→        return []
  1977→
  1978→    # Map LHS signal names to their dependencies
  1979→    signal_to_depth: Dict[str, int] = {}
  1980→
  1981→    # Process in order
  1982→    for lhs, expr in assignments:
  1983→        deps = collect_deps(expr)
  1984→        signal_to_depth[lhs] = deps
  1985→
  1986→    # Compute topological order
  1987→    # Simple iterative approach
  1988→    depth_map: Dict[str, int] = {}
  1989→
  1990→    def get_depth(signal: str) -> int:
  1991→        if signal in depth_map:
  1992→            return depth_map[signal]
  1993→
  1994→        # If it's an input, depth is 0 (we count from input as depth 0)
  1995→        if signal in inputs:
  1996→            return 0
  1997→
  1998→        # If it's an assignment LHS
  1999→        if signal in signal_to_depth:
  2000→            deps = signal_to_depth[signal]
  2001→            if not deps:
  2002→                depth_map[signal] = 1
  2003→                return 1
  2004→
  2005→            max_dep_depth = 0
  2006→            for dep in deps:
  2007→                max_dep_depth = max(max_dep_depth, get_depth(dep))
  2008→
  2009→            depth_map[signal] = max_dep_depth + 1
  2010→            return depth_map[signal]
  2011→
  2012→        # Should not happen for valid circuits
  2013→        return 0
  2014→
  2015→    # Compute depth for all signals that are outputs (directly or indirectly)
  2016→    max_depth = 0
  2017→    for out_name in outputs:
  2018→        max_depth = max(max_depth, get_depth(out_name))
  2019→
  2020→    return max_depth

  2119→class LintWarning:
  2120→    """Base class for lint warnings."""
  2121→    def __init__(self, type_name: str, name: Optional[str], message: str, value: Optional[str] = None):
  2122→        self.type = type_name
  2123→        self.name = name
  2124→        self.message = message
  2125→        self.value = value
  2126→
  2127→    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, object]:
  2128→        result = {
  2129→            'type': self.type,
  2130→            'name': self.name,
  2131→            'message': self.message,
  2132→        }
  2133→        if self.value is not None:
  2134→            result['value'] = self.value
  2135→        return result

  2138→def lint_unused_inputs(declarations: List[SignalDecl],
  2139→                        assignments: List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]]) -> List[LintWarning]:
  2140→    """Find unused input signals."""
  2141→    inputs = {d.name for d in declarations if d.sig_type == 'input'}
  2142→
  2143→    # Collect all signals that are used in assignments
  2144→    used = set()
  2145→    for _, expr in assignments:
  2146→        deps = get_dependencies(expr)
  2147→        for dep in deps:
  2148→            used.add(dep)
  2149→
  2150→    warnings = []
  2151→    for inp in sorted(inputs):
  2152→        if inp not in used:
  2153→            warnings.append(LintWarning(
  2154→                type_name='UnusedInputWarning',
  2155→                name=inp,
  2156→                message=f"input '{inp}' is not used by any output"
  2157→            ))
  2158→
  2159→    return warnings
  2160→
  2161→
  2162→def lint_constant_outputs(declarations: List[SignalDecl],
  2163→                          assignments: List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]],
  2164→                          max_input_bits: int,
  2165→                          filepath: str) -> List[LintWarning]:
  2166→    """Check if outputs are constant. If M > N, skip exhaustive check.
  2167→
  2168→    M = total input bits
  2169→    N = max_input_bits
  2170→    """
  2171→    inputs = {d.name: d for d in declarations if d.sig_type == 'input'}
  2172→    outputs = {d.name: d for d in declarations if d.sig_type == 'output'}
  2173→
  2174→    # Calculate total input bits
  2175→    total_input_bits = sum(d.width for d in inputs.values())
  2176→
  2177→    if total_input_bits > max_input_bits:
  2178→        warnings = [LintWarning(
  2179→            type_name='ConstantOutputCheckSkippedWarning',
  2180→            name=None,
  2181→            message=f"constant output check skipped: {total_input_bits} input bits exceeds limit {max_input_bits}"
  2182→        )]
  2183→        return warnings
  2184→
  2185→    # Build evaluation context
  2186→    signal_widths = {d.name: d.width for d in declarations}
  2187→
  2188→    # Build dependency graph for evaluation
  2189→    graph: Dict[str, List[str]] = {}
  2190→    for lhs, expr in assignments:
  2191→        graph[lhs] = get_dependencies(expr)
  2192→
  2193→    # Evaluate all possible input combinations
  2194→    input_names = sorted(inputs.keys())
  2195→    num_combinations = 1 << total_input_bits
  2196→
  2197→    # For each input combination, evaluate the circuit
  2198→    output_values: Dict[str, Optional[int]] = {name: None for name in outputs}
  2199→    output_widths = {name: d.width for name, d in outputs.items()}
  2200→
  2201→    # Generate all input combinations using bit patterns
  2202→    for pattern in range(num_combinations):
  2203→        input_values = {}
  2204→        # Map bits to inputs
  2205→        bit_pos = 0
  2206→        for inp_name in input_names:
  2207→            width = inputs[inp_name].width
  2208→            # Extract the bits for this input
  2209→            mask = (1 << width) - 1
  2210→            value = (pattern >> bit_pos) & mask
  2211→            input_values[inp_name] = value
  2212→            bit_pos += width
  2213→
  2214→        # Evaluate in topological order
  2215→        values = dict(input_values)
  2216→        max_iterations = len(assignments) * 10
  2217→        for _ in range(max_iterations):
  2218→            progress = False
  2219→            for lhs, expr in assignments:
  2220→                if lhs in values:
  2221→                    continue
  2222→                deps = graph.get(lhs, [])
  2223→                if all(dep in values for dep in deps):
  2224→                    val, width = eval_expression(expr, values, signal_widths, filepath)
  2225→                    values[lhs] = val
  2226→                    progress = True
  2227→
  2228→            if not progress:
  2229→                break
  2230→
  2231→        # Check outputs
  2232→        for out_name in outputs:
  2233→            if out_name not in values:
  2234→                continue
  2235→            val = values[out_name]
  2236→            if output_values[out_name] is None:
  2237→                output_values[out_name] = val
  2238→            elif output_values[out_name] != val:
  2239→                output_values[out_name] = -1  # Mark as non-constant
  2240→
  2241→    warnings = []
  2242→    for out_name, value in output_values.items():
  2243→        if value is not None and value != -1:
  2244→            width = output_widths[out_name]
  2245→            if width == 1:
  2246→                val_str = str(value & 1)
  2247→            else:
  2248→                val_str = f"0b{value:0{width}b}"
  2249→            warnings.append(LintWarning(
  2250→                type_name='ConstantOutputWarning',
  2251→                name=out_name,
  2252→                message=f"output '{out_name}' is constant",
  2253→                value=val_str
  2254→            ))
  2255→
  2256→    return warnings
  2257→

  2346→    """Execute dot command."""
  2347→    # Check file exists first

  2352→                'dot'

  2362→            declarations, assignments = parse_circ_file(filepath)
  2363→        elif fmt == 'json':
  2364→            declarations, assignments = parse_json_file(filepath)
  2365→        elif fmt == 'bench':
  2366→            declarations, assignments = parse_bench_file(filepath)
  2367→        else:
  2368→            raise UnknownInputFormatError(
  2369→                f"Unsupported format: {fmt}",
  2370→                file=filepath
  2371→            )
  2372→
  2373→        result = validate_circ_file(filepath, declarations, assignments)
  2374→        inputs = {d.name: d for d in declarations if d.sig_type == 'input'}
  2375→        outputs = {d.name: d for d in declarations if d.sig_type == 'output'}
  2376→        wires = {d.name: d for d in declarations if d.sig_type == 'wire'}
  2377→
  2378→        # Build dependency graph
  2379→        graph: Dict[str, List[str]] = {}
  2380→        for lhs, expr in assignments:
  2381→            graph[lhs] = get_dependencies(expr)
  2382→
  2383→        # Handle --cone filtering
  2384→        cone_outputs = []
  2385→        if cone:
  2386→            cone_outputs = [name.strip() for name in cone.split(',')]
  2387→            # Verify all cone_outputs are valid outputs
  2388→            for out_name in cone_outputs:
  2389→                if out_name not in outputs:
  2390→                    if json_output:
  2391→                        print(json.dumps({
  2392→                            'ok': False,
  2393→                            'command': 'dot',
  2394→                            'exit_code': 3,
  2395→                            'error': {
  2396→                                'type': 'UndefinedNameError',

  2402→                        }))
  2403→                    else:
